notes: I've played for a few months with Linux. Flawless experience, no issues. Proton-GE, Proton Experimental and a couple other versions, but mostly Experimental. I log in last night to discover my account was suspended with no explanation. I sent an email to EA support appealing the ban and was declined, saying they have confirmation I was using cheating software and it will not be overturned. I (obviously) do not cheat, have no idea how you even would on Linux and have a low K/D regardless. The only explanation I can think of is Proton Experimental had an early change of some sort and flagged the anti-cheat. I would stick to Windows if you take the game seriously.

title: Apex Legends

notes:
YOU HAVE TO TRY AND PLAY FOR 1H TOO SEE THAT GAME IS BORKED ON LINUX AND ONLY CRASHES

All below posts are simply trying to start it and "seems to work" then close it. You have to actually play some time to confirm that it only crashes.

Here is a list of tinkering I did to make the game run even better than it did on Windows 10:

1, Enable ESYNC (ulimit -Hn should be 524288) 
2, Get DXVK WINE Dependencies 
3, Get recommended NVIDIA driver 
3.1, For NVIDIA - OpenGL Settings - Disable "Sync to VBlank" and "Allow Flipping" 
3.2, For NVIDIA - OpenGL Settings - Image Settings "High Performance" 
4, In Steam - Settings - Compatability - Enable "Steam play for all other titles" 
4.1, Choose either Proton Experimental or Proton GE (Download Proton GE if you dont have) 4.2, In settings go to Downloads - Enable "Shader Pre-Caching" 
4.3, In settings go to Downloads - Disable "Allow background processing of Vulkan shaders (seems to be bugged and keeps chugging CPU forever if steam is on but nothing is being played - but if you directly start game after starting steam might actually be fine to keep?) 
5, Install gamemode 
6, Install Apex - Porperties - Launch options - gamemoderun %command% 
6.1, Compatability - Force - Same Proton Above

You could also change to a dedicated gaming kernel like liqorix or Zen - but it seems to only give minimal performance increase
title: Apex Legends

title: Apex Legends

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6900 XT (radeonsi, navi21, LLVM 18.1.8, DRM 3.59, 6.12.1-arch1-1)
notes:
The intro ran very smoothly and I could see no glitches. The anti-cheat appears to be the cause of making it unplayable. 

I tried with Proton 9.0-3 and Proton Experimental

Before the game starts you are presented with a small popup that asks if you just want to start playing or start with DirectX 12 ("Play Apex Legends (DirectX 12)"), I chose the DirectX 12 option - then processing the vulkan shaders took about 40minutes(!!!)
